machineCheckedItems.question: Have you built your frontend using TailwindCSS classes?
Is your frontend code structurally hierarchical using the accurate placement of <h1> to <h6> tags?

findings.message: Heading levels should only increase by one
findings.fix:
Fix any of the following:
  Heading order invalid

findings.message: Page should contain a level-one heading
findings.fix:
Fix all of the following:
  Page must have a level-one heading

machineCheckedItems.question: Are you adding accurate aria attributes to form elements and labels?
Have you tested your frontend code to be AA Level compliant with WCAG 2.1

findings.message: Elements must meet minimum color contrast ratio thresholds
findings.fix:
Fix any of the following:
  Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.88 (foreground color: #9e978f, background color: #ffffff, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: bold). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

findings.message: Images must have alternative text
findings.fix:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have an alt attribute
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

findings.message: Links must have discernible text
findings.fix:
Fix all of the following:
  Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text

Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has an empty title attribute

findings.message: Scrollable region must have keyboard access
findings.fix:
Fix any of the following:
  Element should have focusable content
  Element should be focusable

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you used the login block as described with UAE Pass as the primary login method?
Is the Doctype HTML5 on the top of all pages?
Is the charset declared as UTF-8?
Are you using "rel=canonical" to avoid duplicate content page indexing?

findings.message: No rel=canonical link — review whether duplicate-content indexing is possible on this page.
findings.fix: Add <link rel="canonical" href="…"> when the same content is reachable under several URLs.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured adding the "direction" attribute to your body tag according to your language?

findings.message: Arabic text renders in an LTR context (<h3.heading> "خدمات الشركات") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message:
Arabic text renders in an LTR context (

"ستكشف مجموعة خدمات الشركة المقدمة من قبل منظمتنا.") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message: Arabic text renders in an LTR context (<a.btn-learn-head.mt-3> "اعرف المزيد") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message:
Arabic text renders in an LTR context (

"نتقل إلى دليل خدماتنا واختر الخدمة التي تناسب احتياجاتك بشكل") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message: Arabic text renders in an LTR context (<a.btn-learn-head.mt-3> "اعرف المزيد") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.

findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message: No direction attribute on the document although Arabic content/language is present — RTL is first-class in the standard.
findings.fix: Set dir="rtl" on the Arabic version and dir="ltr" on the English version.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured added the "lang" attribute to your HTML tag based on your language?

findings.message: The document declares lang="en" but the visible text is predominantly Arabic (82% Arabic script) — the declared language must match the content.
findings.fix: Serve the Arabic variant with <html lang="ar" dir="rtl"> (and the English variant with lang="en").

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured all pages list the alternate language tag?

findings.message: No alternate-language link tags (<link rel="alternate" hreflang=…>) — pages should list their other-language version.
findings.fix: Add hreflang alternate links on every page of both language versions.

machineCheckedItems.question: Are all images dynamically adding an alt tag that is meaningful to the image?

findings.message: Images must have alternative text
findings.fix:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have an alt attribute
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

alt="Image Placeholder" on 16 image(s) (e.g. src="https://apps.fnrc.gov.ae/website_AdminPanel/Content/Dynamic-Content/Banner-Image") looks like a CMS placeholder or filename, not a description — it passes automated alt checks while conveying nothing (WCAG 1.1.1). Review and replace with real alternative text.
findings.fix: Write alternative text that describes the image's content or function; use alt="" only for purely decorative images.

findings.message: alt="Alternate Text" on 6 image(s) (e.g. src="https://apps.fnrc.gov.ae/website_AdminPanel/Content/Dynamic-Content/Banner-Image") looks like a CMS placeholder or filename, not a description — it passes automated alt checks while conveying nothing (WCAG 1.1.1). Review and replace with real alternative text.
findings.fix: Write alternative text that describes the image's content or function; use alt="" only for purely decorative images.

disclaimers: Community tool. Not affiliated with or endorsed by TDRA.
Automated checks cover only a machine-checkable subset of the standard — a clean automated run is NOT compliance and NOT WCAG 2.1/2.2 AA conformance.
Arabic/RTL parity findings are flags for native-speaker review; Mizan never asserts parity as settled.
Lighthouse numbers were produced under the run conditions recorded in this report (local machine, simulated throttling) — they are comparable across local runs, not to TDRA's environment.
This is a private report for the site's own team.
